pattern: Placing the most frequent values first into alternating slots guarantees identical values never land adjacent.
hints: If any value appeared more than half the time you could not separate it, but the problem promises a valid arrangement exists.
Fill positions in the order 0, 2, 4, ... then 1, 3, 5, ... so that copies of the same value are spread out.
Order values by descending frequency, then drop them into the even indices first and the odd indices next, exhausting each value before moving on.

edge_inputs.description: one value dominates up to the limit
exactly half are the same value
two values with one extra of the majority

stress.description: large multiset where one value is near the maximum allowed frequency
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 600)))
    half = (n + 1) // 2
    major = rng.randint(1, 10000)
    arr = [major] * min(half, n)
    others = [v for v in range(1, 10001) if v != major]
    rng.shuffle(others)
    idx = 0
    while len(arr) < n:
        arr.append(others[idx % len(others)])
        idx += 1
    rng.shuffle(arr)
    return [arr]
stress.note: majority value capped at ceil(n/2) so a valid arrangement always exists
